title: Euzomodendron longirostre
intro: Euzomodendron longirostre, also known as the Long-beaked Euzomodendron, is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. The plant is rich in various bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as cardiovascular protection and immune system modulation. The bioactive constituents of Euzomodendron longirostre may also contribute to its traditional use in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. In traditional herbal medicine, Euzomodendron longirostre is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in the production of tonics and other herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Euzomodendron longirostre is classified within the family Santalaceae, which is a family of parasitic plants commonly known as sandalwoods. The genus Euzomodendron is a monotypic genus, containing only one species. This plant is native to China and is considered a rare and endangered species. It is a type of hemiparasite, deriving some of its nutrients from the surrounding environment. Euzomodendron longirostre is also known as the 'Chinese mistletoe'.
